method_boundary.M1: source/dossier prompt-layer evidence; direct prompt patchability differs by harness.
method_boundary.M2: source/dossier/tool-surface evidence; direct tool-definition patchability differs by harness.
method_boundary.M3: executed counterfactual runs in repeat ranges 301-312 and 401/403/404.
method_boundary.M4: trace/planning-loop visibility evidence from public traces and private audits; hidden chain-of-thought omitted.

methods.finding: same model, different harness prompt/instruction stacks; prompt-layer differences are a plausible main-effect source.
methods.left_harness_note: agent/provider/config metadata is visible, but full system prompt text is only partially observable.
methods.name: system prompt layer attribution
methods.right_harness_note: system prompt layers are visible through session JSON and source-level prompt builder evidence.

methods.finding: different harness tool surfaces and affordances; observed sequence shift aligns with tool-definition/tool-surface differences.
methods.left_harness_note: tool calls and provider/agent permissions are visible in JSON/export traces; complete native tool registry internals are partial.
methods.name: tool definition and affordance attribution
methods.right_harness_note: tool registry/toolset composition is source-derived and session JSON exposes model-visible tool schemas.

methods.finding: direct target-file disclosure changed tool strategy but did not reduce the success gap.
methods.name: direct target-file counterfactual

methods.finding: trace exposes chosen tool path and reasoning markers; hidden chain-of-thought remains omitted by policy.
methods.left_harness_note: JSON trace exposes text/reasoning markers/tool_use events and usage; full prompt visibility is lower than Claude/Codex/Hermes.
methods.name: planning-loop trace attribution
methods.right_harness_note: session JSON records system prompt presence, tools, messages, tool calls, and reasoning markers; provider raw payload is not committed.

methods.finding: baseline prompt says to keep a negative sign but does not explicitly specify whether the sign belongs before or after `$`.
methods.name: task/prompt convention ambiguity

methods.finding: tool surfaces can edit either convention; the direct-file and semantic counterfactuals isolate output semantics from tool availability.
methods.name: tool affordance non-cause

methods.finding: disclosing the `$-12.50` project convention flips both sides to success
methods.name: negative-currency convention counterfactual

methods.finding: private audits show visible outputs using `-$...` in direct-file runs and passing traces after `$-...` convention disclosure.
methods.name: visible trace/output convention evidence
